in its various forms and some years quite a few more. No advertising, no marketing, no promotion, no nothing and frankly not a very good title. Some people get confused with Scientology, some people are frightened by it, it doesn't say what it is, I mean it does but you kind of got to know Latin and you got to think about it a little bit so you know that but still. So why does this book primarily through word of mouth, well because it's really, it delivers a really simple certainty and that is think better about yourself and everything else will work out. You'll automatically have more money, you'll get more cooperation from people, everybody will love you more, all you got to do is think better about yourself. So he gives a real simple certainty to a whole collection of frankly very complex problems and the world has responded well to the message. So this principle of desires met is about understanding the general and generic and key emotional desires of people and then figuring out a way to give them what they want. So you look at page 113. Here's a real good quote from my quote file, you don't influence with information, you don't influence with education. Now a bunch of us spent a lot of time trying to do that usually in great futility because all you do with information is you inform, you don't necessarily influence so you have a better informed person, you may have a smarter person but you don't necessarily have somebody, all of the things we said earlier bonded to you and willing to march into battle for you and so on. With education all you do is educate. The truth is they want things to believe in, they don't necessarily want to know anymore, they don't necessarily want to be smarter, they want things to believe in and would prefer not to be very analytical about it and that's both good news and bad news depending upon how you look at this but it is important to understand about people that not only they want simple concepts but they want faith and so they want the magic talisman that they can carry with them that protects them from evil, they want the magic pill that will cause the pounds to roll off while they sleep at night and because it has a formulaic pharmaceutical sounding name and it contains bat-dung as its magic ingredient therefore we can believe in it and so that's what they're looking for. Now the question is how far can you go and will you go to deliver it but the thing to understand is that when we inform and educate we are not building our influence over people. You would think you are but you're not. You may be helping them, you may be delivering value to them but you're not building your influence over them. When you give them things to believe in then you are building your influence. There's a corral, there's a few pages here which you can read again at your own leisure and so if you skip to 119 the extension of this then is so on one hand there's this set of sort of general broad emotional and psychological desires of people that we need to fulfill. Then there's the principle of specific desires and specific desires now differ for everybody's customers in the room so everybody's are different. There's a broad list of general things that apply to all but then there are some very specific things that apply to each and more effort is invested in trying to sell things to people trying to persuade people to do something trying to influence people then first understanding the people we are trying to influence and you guys are better than most right out of the gate doing this you're further along but usually and you can try it with your own clients if you help them market as well. Most people cannot thoroughly describe the people they're trying to influence. They don't have a really good profile of the person that they are that they are trying to affect. They don't have a good psychological profile, they don't have a good emotional profile, they don't have really an even an experiential profile you know what that person's day to day existence is like. If you could follow your typical customer around for a month live in their house over here their conversations take notes on it all you would view that customer very differently than the way that you view them now and so the building of the profile of the person you are attempting to influence will now work backwards to help with everything that we've talked about all day and hardly anybody spends enough time on it. I've given you some information and examples so back from Made to Stick if you look at page 120 there's a page out of there which describes the brilliant work done by Rick Warren for the Saddleback Church and this page describes their building of a profile of the person who they call Saddleback Sam that they wish to attract to this church and it makes the point there's an example there for you but it makes the point that their actual profile goes into much greater depth and the key line by making Saddleback Sam and Samantha a living breathing concrete presence that gives them great power now in attempting to influence these people. There's also a really good word used by the Made to Stick guys called abstract speak and so most people have this abstract idea of who their intended target is usually defined demographically age gender income etc but they don't have this kind of a profile you know I've written a lot of low-end opportunity stuff kind of the places where TJ lives and TV handed us the person for that so every time I've been writing that stuff I've been writing for Al Bundy the character in Married with Children he's the buyer of the typical low-end get-rich opportunity and so there's pretty clear profile of Al Bundy there's five years of television you can kind of look at him and see how he behaves and how he thinks and how he talks and that's exactly our guy most businesses you probably haven't been handed a living breathing profile like that to look at and so you have to think about it and you have to build it and so what they've done with creating this Saddleback Sam is they've created their Al Bundy they've created the person who they are talking to and attempting to influence on page 121 there's another example of a profiling tool and so this is this is called the emotional mini-bio now there's only part of the game is the emotions but you'll see for example philosophy questions like what is this person's underlying attitude about life so if you had your customer describe their philosophy to you like we examined philosophy early what would your customers philosophy be about money how would they express it what would your customers philosophy be about politics how would they express it what would your customers philosophy be about time and how would they express it right here's a really good question what does she need to know about another person in order to accept that other as trustworthy
